summary: UR273A is the SPARSEST event register in the analysed Incahuasi set: 87 cords (83 L1 + 4 subsidiaries), 64 EMPTY (74 percent), 34 clusters fragmented by the empties. Provenance: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 14 -- same unidad-block as UR266, UR270, UR278, UR268, UR272, UR275, UR277. Physically DUAL-BOUND to UR273B at BOTH primary cords -- a KB cord wraps around both PCs, then a NB-W cord ties both PCs with a shared subsidiary, making cord 1 of UR273A and cord 1 of UR273B the SAME COMPOUND CORD. The binding is stronger than the one-end wraps of UR267A/B or UR255/256. Despite the sparsity v3 unlocks 9 vocabulary occurrences (v1: 1) revealing the COMPLETE cadastral formula compressed into p40-p63 at 3-cord cadence: waqa (p40) - qaqa (p48) - chaqa (p51) - kaqa (p54) - chiqa (p57) - taqa (p60) - paqa (p63). 7 of 8 canonical cadastral markers (only siqa absent). A delayed doubled-seal closes at p69 chiqa + p74 chaqa. No KFG Ascher twin (pendant-pendant sums page returns 404 for KH0510) -- no arithmetic verification layer, consistent with an event-log primary register rather than an audit. The sparsity + compressed cadastral identity + dual binding = EVENT REGISTER for a specific parcel cluster: UR273A logs the events, UR273B likely carries the audit totals.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0510 = UR273A. 87 cords (83 L1 + 4 subsidiaries).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, relational depth L2.
  34 clusters (heavily fragmented by 64 empty cords).
  5 colors: NB(42, 48%), W(31, 36%), KB(6, 7%), ?(5), MB(3, 3%). NB-dominant.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 14 -- the main Incahuasi archive block.
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'Two Khipus tied to each other by cords on both primary cords.
  Named UR273A and UR273B. Both Beginnings are tied around by a KB cord, after which there is a
  NB-W cord tying both PCs; has a subsidiary. This cord will be #1 on both khipus. Cord starts with
  two markers: KB marker around PC of both Khipus and NB-W around PC of both khipus.'
  The DOUBLE BINDING (KB cord + NB-W cord, both wrapping both primary cords + shared subsidiary) is
  STRONGER than the one-end wraps of UR267A/B or UR255/256 -- UR273A + UR273B are an INSEPARABLE UNIT.
  Not individually discussed in Urton 2017.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  Most cords resolved via KFG; 9 L? remain.
  v1 -> v3: 1 -> 9 STRING readings (+8), 1 -> 7 distinct vocabulary (+6).
  Old v1 called this 'sparse event register with single qaqa'; v3 reveals the FULL cadastral formula
  compressed into p40-p74.

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT APPLICABLE. KFG pendant-pendant sums page returned 404 for KH0510. No arithmetic layer available.
  The absence of Ascher sums is CONSISTENT with UR273A being an EVENT REGISTER (primary log) rather
  than an audit or summary document.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- SPARSE EVENT LOG
  74 percent empty cords (64/87) = sparsest Incahuasi document analysed.
  14 INT values scattered across 87 positions. Range 30-891, no positional constant (no 10/15/47/208).
  9 STRING cords compressed into p40-p74 = SINGLE TEXT BLOCK of 34 cords containing all vocabulary.
  Opening (p1-p39): almost all empty or L? on NB/W/KB. Pre-formatted register awaiting entries.
  Text block (p40-p74): vocabulary-dense zone.
  Closure (p75-p83): again mostly empty.
  Cross-khipu test (exploratory): UR273A vs UR278 running sums 10/12 match; vs UR270 6/12. NOT decisive
  (UR266's UR278 match was 7/7 at p-positions with null p<10^-5; UR273A's matches are softer).
  UR273A does NOT cross-audit another khipu in the UR266 style.

VOCABULARY (7 words, 9 occurrences, 100 percent dict hit)
  chaqa x2 (bridge / delivery)
  chiqa x2 (truth / verification)
  waqa  x1 (huaca / sacred place)
  qaqa  x1 (rock / landmark)
  kaqa  x1 (wall)
  taqa  x1 (boundary)
  paqa  x1 (east / dawn)
  Full cadastral signature: 7 of 8 canonical markers. Missing: siqa (no ramp).
  DENSE CADASTRAL FORMULA compressed into narrow p40-p74 text block.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p40 W  waqa  -- HUACA WITNESS
  p48 W  qaqa  -- rock landmark
  p51 NB chaqa -- bridge / delivery (only vocab on NB)
  p54 W  kaqa  -- wall
  p57 W  chiqa -- verification stone
  p60 W  taqa  -- boundary
  p63 W  paqa  -- east origin
  p69 W  chiqa -- DOUBLED VERIFICATION
  p74 W  chaqa -- DOUBLED DELIVERY
  Cadence p40-p63: 7 cadastral markers at 3-cord intervals.
  Terminal pair (p69 chiqa + p74 chaqa): delayed doubled-seal.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  NB (42, 48%) = DOMINANT STRUCTURAL CHANNEL -- most empties + single chaqa at p51.
  W  (31, 36%) = PRIMARY VOCABULARY carrier -- 8 of 9 vocab on W.
  KB (6), MB (3), ? (5) = minor.
  Inverted from UR267A/UR255 (W-dominant data) and UR267B/UR256 (W+AB): UR273A is NB-dominant. NB
  carries the PRE-FORMATTED empty structure, W the ACTUAL DATA + vocabulary.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR273A is a PREPARED REGISTER FORM: 34 cluster-slots set up in advance and mostly left unfilled.
  The 39-cord opening block is almost pure placeholder. At p40 the scribe compresses the COMPLETE
  parcel-naming formula into 23 cord positions: 7 of 8 canonical cadastral markers at 3-cord cadence.
  Then the document closes with a doubled verification + delivery seal at p69+p74 and 10 more empties.
  The KB + NB-W double-binding with UR273B at both ends makes the pair inseparable: UR273A is the
  SPARSE EVENT LOG, UR273B likely the FULL AUDIT of events tracked against the naming formula here.
narrative:
Most of this document is blank. Sixty-four cords out of eighty-seven are empty knots, prepared positions never filled. Eighty-seven cords were spun and tied to the primary cord, but only nine of them carry words, and only fourteen carry numbers. The Inca scribe who built UR273A left it mostly empty.

And yet the first thing that this register does is physically bind itself to another khipu -- UR273B, the companion -- at the primary cord. A KB cord wraps both primary cords together; a NB-W cord runs through both, with a shared subsidiary. The first cord of UR273A is the first cord of UR273B. They were meant to be read as one double document.

For thirty-nine cords, almost nothing. Scattered numbers on alpaca-brown and white: two hundred seventy-two, two hundred ninety-seven, eight hundred eight, one hundred twenty-six, eight hundred ninety-one. No pattern. Placeholders waiting for entries that never came.

Then at cord forty, on white, the first word: waqa -- the huaca. A sacred place is named. At cord forty-eight, qaqa -- the rock. The landmark of a specific field. At cord fifty-one, on near-black, chaqa -- delivery. At fifty-four on white, kaqa -- the terrace wall. At fifty-seven, chiqa -- boundary stone, verified. At sixty, taqa -- the frontier. At sixty-three, paqa -- east.

In seven cords, across twenty-three cord-positions, the scribe has compressed the complete cadastral formula: huaca, rock, delivery, wall, verification, boundary, east. Seven of the eight canonical markers. This is how an Incahuasi field is NAMED in full: the sacred place that presides, the rock that anchors it, the delivery that binds it to the imperial economy, the wall that defines its terrace, the verification stone, the frontier, the direction of observation.

At cord sixty-nine, chiqa again: a re-verification. At cord seventy-four, chaqa again: the delivery re-confirmed. Then ten more empty cords, the document closing without another word.

UR273A is not a ledger. It is a REGISTER FORM -- the Inca equivalent of a printed form with blank lines -- prepared with thirty-four cluster-slots and left almost entirely unfilled. The vocabulary that appears compresses a complete land-naming formula into a single text block, as if the scribe needed to establish the parcel's full identity once and then log the sparse events against that identity. The companion UR273B, tied to this document at the origin, was likely the one that actually held the audit totals -- making UR273A the sparse event log and UR273B the full accounting.

prior_work.claim: Incahuasi Sector A Subsector 1 UA 4 Unidad 20N (UE 14) is the main storeroom block of the Inkawasi archive, housing the dense khipu set studied by Urton and Chu.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4); Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports.

prior_work.claim: Canonical Andean cadastral vocabulary includes waqa (huaca), qaqa (rock), chaqa (bridge / delivery), kaqa (wall), chiqa (verification), taqa (boundary), paqa (east / dawn), siqa (ramp / climb).
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario; Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario Aymara; Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo.

prior_work.claim: Dual-bound Incahuasi khipu pairs (UR267A/B, UR255/256) were first identified as architectural co-units in the Urton/Chu excavation records; UR273A/B is a stronger-bound member of this class.
prior_work.citation: OKR cord-notes for UR273A and UR273B (Urton); Urton 2017 Inka History in Knots.

v3_contribution.claim: COMPLETE CADASTRAL FORMULA (7 of 8 canonical markers) compressed into 23 cord-positions at 3-cord cadence: waqa (p40) -> qaqa (p48) -> chaqa (p51) -> kaqa (p54) -> chiqa (p57) -> taqa (p60) -> paqa (p63). The only missing marker is siqa (ramp). This is the DENSEST cadastral-identity block in the analysed Incahuasi set.

evidence.test: 7/8 canonical cadastral markers in p40-p63 at 3-cord cadence

v3_contribution.claim: DELAYED DOUBLED-SEAL: chiqa at p69 + chaqa at p74 = verification + delivery RE-ASSERTED after the cadastral block. The doubled-seal is the same convention as UR267B's chiqa-chiqa double and UR256's chaqa-chaqa double, but applied at wider spacing (5-cord gap rather than adjacent).

SPARSEST DOCUMENT in the analysed set: 74 percent empty cords (64/87). The prepared but mostly-unfilled form is consistent with an EVENT REGISTER set up for events expected to occur but largely un-recorded -- a garrison-administration contingency register.

evidence.test: empty-cord fraction across Incahuasi v3 set

v3_contribution.claim: STRONGEST DUAL BINDING in the Incahuasi set: KB cord + NB-W cord BOTH wrap both primary cords of UR273A and UR273B, with a shared subsidiary. The pair's cord 1 is a SINGLE COMPOUND CORD. Stronger than UR267A/B or UR255/256 which bind only at one end.

evidence.test: both-ends KB + NB-W double binding per OKR physical description

v3_contribution.claim: COLOR-ROLE INVERSION: UR273A is NB-dominant (48 percent) where most Incahuasi commodity khipus are W-dominant. NB carries the PRE-FORMATTED empty structure; W carries ACTUAL DATA + 8 of 9 vocabulary cords. The colour-channel split is itself the form/data distinction.

evidence.test: NB-dominant structural-carrier vs W-dominant data-carrier cross-khipu comparison

v3_contribution.claim: NO ASCHER LAYER: KFG pendant-pendant sums page returns 404 for KH0510. The absence of arithmetic cross-check is CONSISTENT with an event-log primary register rather than an audit document. No Pass 3A / 3C / 3D readings possible.

UR273A / UR273B pairing hypothesis: UR273A holds the sparse event log (vocabulary-present, numbers-sparse, no Ascher); UR273B likely holds the audit totals for events logged against the cadastral identity established at p40-p63. Testable by deep-read of UR273B.

companions.note: Physically inseparable companion: KH0511, 124 cords, dual-bound to UR273A at BOTH primary cords by KB + NB-W wraps with shared subsidiary. UR273A cord 1 = UR273B cord 1 (single compound cord). UR273B likely carries the audit totals for events logged in UR273A's sparse register.

evidence.test: both-ends KB + NB-W double-wrap (OKR physical note)

companions.note: UE 19 peanut audit cadastral map. Shares the full cadastral vocabulary (paqa-qaqa-kaqa-taqa-chaqa-waqa-chiqa) but at lower density and WITH 47-peanut positional constant. UR273A's compressed formula is the naming convention, UR275 the audit-tier application.

UE 22 audit ledger (reclassified from astronomical). Shares the sparse-log signature (~41 percent empty) but without UR273A's strong dual binding. UR272 + UR273A are two instances of the Incahuasi sparse-event-log convention.
UE 16 aji tier-1 day-book. Dual-bound to UR267B at BEGINNING ONLY (single-end wrap) -- UR273A's both-ends wrap is the stronger class of the same convention.
UE 21 aji Sector C field book. Also single-end dual-bound (to UR256). UR273A/B stands as the strong-binding member of the same paired-document convention.

UE 12 cross-khipu audit of UR278. UR266 achieved a decisive 7/7 cross-khipu match with UR278 running sums. UR273A's exploratory 10/12 match with UR278 is SOFT, not decisive -- UR273A does NOT cross-audit another document in the UR266 style.
UE 16 commodity matrix. Exploratory running-sum match with UR273A (10/12) is soft and not directly decisive; UR273A is unlikely to be UR278's audit companion.

word_reading.note: 9/9 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent dict hit). All 7 canonical cadastral markers are stable and frequently attested across the corpus; the terminal doubled-seal (chiqa@p69 + chaqa@p74) is paleographically consistent with UR267B and UR256 doubled-seal conventions.

document_type.note: event_register classification strongly supported: 74 percent empty (prepared-but-unfilled form) + full cadastral-identity compressed block + strong dual binding + no Ascher layer = garrison-style contingency event-log signature. Sub-type consistent with parcel-cluster event register tied to a companion audit khipu (UR273B).

narrative.note: The prepared-form reading is internally coherent and directly supported by (a) the 74 percent empty cord count, (b) the compressed cadastral identity block, (c) the dual binding to UR273B as the pair's audit companion. The 'garrison contingency' flavour of the interpretation is plausible but not decisive; a deep-read of UR273B would confirm the event-log + audit-totals pairing.

data_integrity.note: No Ascher layer available (KFG page 404 for KH0510). 9 L? cords remain unresolved. Knot-level data partially resolved via KFG merge. No arithmetic cross-check possible; integrity relies on knot-level reading and the physical double-binding evidence.

sections.title: Dual-Bound Origin
sections.reading: The primary cord is a SHARED compound cord with UR273B. A KB cord wraps both PCs; a NB-W cord ties both PCs with a shared subsidiary. UR273A cord 1 = UR273B cord 1. Both ends wrap = strongest dual binding in the analysed set.

key_cords.note: DUAL BINDING with UR273B -- both primary cords are the same compound cord

sections.title: Opening Empty Placeholder Block
sections.reading: Thirty-nine cords of almost nothing. 5 scattered INT values (272, 297, 808, 126, 891) on NB/W; the rest empty or L?. Pre-formatted slots awaiting entries that never came. NB-dominant structural channel carries the blank register form.

key_cords.note: first scattered INT -- register setup data
largest scattered INT in the opening block

sections.title: Compressed Cadastral Identity Formula
sections.reading: The COMPLETE parcel-naming formula compressed into 23 cord-positions at 3-cord cadence. Seven of the eight canonical cadastral markers: waqa (huaca witness) -> qaqa (rock landmark) -> chaqa (bridge/delivery, on NB) -> kaqa (wall) -> chiqa (verification stone) -> taqa (boundary) -> paqa (east origin). Only siqa (ramp) missing. This is how an Incahuasi field is NAMED IN FULL.

key_cords.note: HUACA WITNESS -- first word, opens the cadastral block
bridge / delivery -- only vocab on NB (structural color)

sections.title: Delayed Doubled Seal
sections.reading: After the cadastral block the scribe leaves five empties then re-asserts the verification and delivery. At p69 chiqa (doubled verification); at p74 chaqa (doubled delivery). The delayed doubled-seal parallels UR267B's chiqa-chiqa and UR256's chaqa-chaqa adjacent doubles, but applied at wider spacing.

sections.title: Closing Empty Tail
sections.reading: Ten more empty cords close the document. No further vocabulary, no numerical register. The form ends as it began: blank lines waiting for entries.

key_cords.note: LAST CORD -- document closes with empty tail
